_notes: All roles default to GEMINI_API_KEY -- one free key is enough at a few-hours-between-turns cadence. GM/God/DM adjudication use gemini-3.5-flash AND a real ('medium') thinking_level since they carry the most judgment load (following 'don't reveal unless triggered', telling confabulation apart from legitimate divergent belief) -- these are exactly the tasks Gemini's own docs say benefit from actual reasoning, not the ones to strip it from. Players use gemini-3.1-flash-lite with 'minimal' thinking since choosing an action is comparatively low-complexity and a wrong guess there just becomes an in-character choice, not a broken pipeline. Gemini 3.x thinking tokens share the SAME max_output_tokens budget as the visible answer (combined, not separate) -- max_tokens here has real headroom above the schema's typical output size specifically so a role's thinking can't silently truncate its own JSON (JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string is the symptom if this budget is too tight). Both models are free-tier as of writing -- Gemini's free-tier model list changes often, so if a model name here 404s, check the current model list rather than assuming the code broke. To split quota across separate keys later: change a role's api_key_env to something like GEMINI_API_KEY_PLAYER_A and add that as a second GitHub secret -- but remember a second key only helps if it's from a different Google Cloud project than the first.
